CHEP 898 Supervised Learning

Harsimran Ghai

2026-03-21

Loading the Library

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(tidymodels)
## ── Attaching packages ────────────────────────────────────── tidymodels 1.4.1 ──
## ✔ broom        1.0.11     ✔ rsample      1.3.2 
## ✔ dials        1.4.2      ✔ tailor       0.1.0 
## ✔ infer        1.1.0      ✔ tune         2.0.1 
## ✔ modeldata    1.5.1      ✔ workflows    1.3.0 
## ✔ parsnip      1.4.1      ✔ workflowsets 1.1.1 
## ✔ recipes      1.3.1      ✔ yardstick    1.3.2 
## ── Conflicts ───────────────────────────────────────── tidymodels_conflicts() ──
## ✖ scales::discard() masks purrr::discard()
## ✖ dplyr::filter()   masks stats::filter()
## ✖ recipes::fixed()  masks stringr::fixed()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()      masks stats::lag()
## ✖ yardstick::spec() masks readr::spec()
## ✖ recipes::step()   masks stats::step()
library(sjPlot)
## 
## Attaching package: 'sjPlot'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     set_theme
library(C50)
library(ranger)
library(finalfit)
library(knitr)
library(gtsummary)
library(mlbench)
library(vip)
## 
## Attaching package: 'vip'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:utils':
## 
##     vi
library(rsample)
library(rpart.plot)
## Loading required package: rpart
## 
## Attaching package: 'rpart'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:dials':
## 
##     prune
library(tune)
library(recipes)
library(yardstick)
library(parsnip)
library(glmnet)
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
## 
## Loaded glmnet 4.1-10
library(themis)
library(microbenchmark)

Loading the Data

I used the Canpath Imputed dataset for this assignment.

data<-read_csv("Canpath_imputed.csv")
## Rows: 41187 Columns: 93
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr  (1): ID
## dbl (92): ADM_STUDY_ID, SDC_GENDER, SDC_AGE_CALC, SDC_MARITAL_STATUS, SDC_ED...
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Simplifying the dataset

Extracting some of the relevant variables to make the dataset simple for ease of computation.

Small_data <- select(data, DIS_CROHN_EVER, HS_GEN_HEALTH, SDC_AGE_CALC, SDC_EDU_LEVEL, PM_BMI_SR, WRK_FULL_TIME, SMK_CIG_EVER, SDC_INCOME, PA_TOTAL_SHORT, HS_ROUTINE_VISIT_EVER, PSE_ADULT_WRK_DURATION, DIS_RESP_SLEEP_APNEA_EVER, SDC_EDU_LEVEL_AGE, SDC_GENDER)

Preparing the variables

The Crohn Disease was used as the outcome variable of interest. The variables was recoded to binary with 0 representing no crohn disease and 1 representing crohn disease.

table(Small_data$DIS_CROHN_EVER)
## 
##     0     1     2 
## 38857   348  1982
Small_data <- Small_data %>%
    mutate(Crohn = case_when(
        DIS_CROHN_EVER == 0 ~ "No",
        DIS_CROHN_EVER == 1 ~ "Yes",
        DIS_CROHN_EVER == 2 ~ "No")) %>%
        mutate(Crohn = as.factor(Crohn))

table(Small_data$DIS_CROHN_EVER, Small_data$Crohn)
##    
##        No   Yes
##   0 38857     0
##   1     0   348
##   2  1982     0
Small_data$DIS_CROHN_EVER <- NULL

The general health variable was recoded from 5 levels to 4 levels to make the sample even in each sample.

table(Small_data$HS_GEN_HEALTH)
## 
##     1     2     3     4     5 
##   832  3377 12783 17183  7012
# Merging the level 1 and level 2 to make the level close in sample size

Small_data <- Small_data %>%
    mutate(gen_health = case_when(
        HS_GEN_HEALTH == 1 ~ 1,
        HS_GEN_HEALTH == 2 ~ 1,
        HS_GEN_HEALTH == 3 ~ 2,
        HS_GEN_HEALTH == 4 ~ 3, 
        HS_GEN_HEALTH == 5 ~ 4)) %>%
        mutate(gen_health = as.factor(gen_health))

table(Small_data$HS_GEN_HEALTH, Small_data$gen_health)
##    
##         1     2     3     4
##   1   832     0     0     0
##   2  3377     0     0     0
##   3     0 12783     0     0
##   4     0     0 17183     0
##   5     0     0     0  7012
Small_data$HS_GEN_HEALTH <- NULL

Exploratory data Analysis

Changing variables into factors

Small_data <- Small_data %>% mutate(across(c(SDC_EDU_LEVEL,WRK_FULL_TIME, SMK_CIG_EVER, SDC_INCOME, HS_ROUTINE_VISIT_EVER, DIS_RESP_SLEEP_APNEA_EVER,SDC_GENDER),as.factor))

Descriptive of dataset

summary(Small_data)
##   SDC_AGE_CALC   SDC_EDU_LEVEL     PM_BMI_SR     WRK_FULL_TIME SMK_CIG_EVER
##  Min.   :30.00   6      :11010   Min.   : 8.86   0:18880       0:22255     
##  1st Qu.:43.00   4      :10175   1st Qu.:23.41   1:22307       1:18932     
##  Median :52.00   2      : 7809   Median :26.62                             
##  Mean   :51.48   7      : 6268   Mean   :27.66                             
##  3rd Qu.:60.00   3      : 3486   3rd Qu.:30.68                             
##  Max.   :74.00   5      : 1795   Max.   :69.40                             
##                  (Other):  644                                             
##    SDC_INCOME   PA_TOTAL_SHORT  HS_ROUTINE_VISIT_EVER PSE_ADULT_WRK_DURATION
##  6      :8826   Min.   :    0   0:  536               Min.   : 0.000        
##  4      :8008   1st Qu.:  600   1:40651               1st Qu.: 0.000        
##  5      :7667   Median : 1800                         Median : 2.000        
##  3      :6815   Mean   : 2606                         Mean   : 6.687        
##  7      :3965   3rd Qu.: 3795                         3rd Qu.:10.000        
##  8      :2885   Max.   :19278                         Max.   :51.000        
##  (Other):3021                                                               
##  DIS_RESP_SLEEP_APNEA_EVER SDC_EDU_LEVEL_AGE SDC_GENDER Crohn       gen_health
##  0:35355                   Min.   :-7.00     1:15200    No :40839   1: 4209   
##  1: 3916                   1st Qu.:19.00     2:25987    Yes:  348   2:12783   
##  2: 1916                   Median :23.00                            3:17183   
##                            Mean   :25.34                            4: 7012   
##                            3rd Qu.:28.00                                      
##                            Max.   :73.00                                      
##

Checking Duplicates

duplicated(Small_data)

## [41005] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41017] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41029] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41041] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41053] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41065] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41077] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41089] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41101] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41113] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41125] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41137] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41149] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41161] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41173] FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE FALSE
## [41185] FALSE FALSE FALSE

No Duplicated values were found in this dataset.

Checking the missing values

any(is.na(Small_data))
## [1] FALSE

No missing values were found in the dataset as expected from imputed dataset.

Splitting the dataset

The dataset was split to evaluate the model performance after fitting the model on training dataset.

# Fixing the random numbers by setting the seed to reproduce the analysis
set.seed(10)

# Cross Validation Split
cv_split <- initial_validation_split(Small_data, 
                            strata = Crohn, 
                            prop = c(0.70, 0.20))

# Create data frames for the two sets:
train_data <- training(cv_split)
table(train_data$Crohn)
## 
##    No   Yes 
## 28587   243
test_data  <- testing(cv_split)
table(test_data$Crohn)
## 
##   No  Yes 
## 4084   35

Model Building

Setting the core

cores <- parallel::detectCores()
cores
## [1] 10
rf_model <- rand_forest(mtry = tune(), min_n = tune(), trees = 100) %>% 
              set_engine("ranger", num.threads = cores) %>% 
              set_mode("classification")

Creating a Recipe

Health_recipe <- 
  recipe(Crohn ~ ., data = train_data) %>% 
  step_zv(all_predictors())

Creating a Workflow

Health_workflow <- 
        workflow() %>% 
        add_model(rf_model) %>% 
        add_recipe(Health_recipe)

Health_workflow
## ══ Workflow ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Preprocessor: Recipe
## Model: rand_forest()
## 
## ── Preprocessor ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## 1 Recipe Step
## 
## • step_zv()
## 
## ── Model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Random Forest Model Specification (classification)
## 
## Main Arguments:
##   mtry = tune()
##   trees = 100
##   min_n = tune()
## 
## Engine-Specific Arguments:
##   num.threads = cores
## 
## Computational engine: ranger

Fitting the Model

Hyperparameter Tuning

set.seed(100)

folds <- vfold_cv(train_data, v = 10) 

rf_grid <- grid_regular(
              mtry(range = c(2, 13)),
              min_n(range = c(5, 50)),
              levels = 5  
            )

rf_grid
## # A tibble: 25 × 2
##     mtry min_n
##    <int> <int>
##  1     2     5
##  2     4     5
##  3     7     5
##  4    10     5
##  5    13     5
##  6     2    16
##  7     4    16
##  8     7    16
##  9    10    16
## 10    13    16
## # ℹ 15 more rows
Health_fit <- tune_grid(
                Health_workflow,
                resamples = folds,
                grid = rf_grid, 
                control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE, 
                                                  verbose = FALSE))

Health_fit
## # Tuning results
## # 10-fold cross-validation 
## # A tibble: 10 × 5
##    splits               id     .metrics          .notes           .predictions
##    <list>               <chr>  <list>            <list>           <list>      
##  1 <split [25947/2883]> Fold01 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  2 <split [25947/2883]> Fold02 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  3 <split [25947/2883]> Fold03 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  4 <split [25947/2883]> Fold04 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  5 <split [25947/2883]> Fold05 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  6 <split [25947/2883]> Fold06 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  7 <split [25947/2883]> Fold07 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  8 <split [25947/2883]> Fold08 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
##  9 <split [25947/2883]> Fold09 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>    
## 10 <split [25947/2883]> Fold10 <tibble [75 × 6]> <tibble [0 × 4]> <tibble>

Plotting of the results.

Health_fit %>%
  collect_metrics() %>%
  filter(.metric == "accuracy") %>%
  select(mean, min_n, mtry) %>%
  pivot_longer(min_n:mtry,
    values_to = "value",
    names_to = "parameter"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(value, mean, color = parameter)) +
  geom_point(show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~parameter, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Accuracy")

Health_fit %>%
  collect_metrics() %>%
  filter(.metric == "accuracy") %>%
  mutate(min_n = factor(min_n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(mtry, mean, color = min_n)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(y = "Accuracy")
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Collecting the results

rf_best <- 
  Health_fit %>% 
  select_best(metric = "accuracy")

rf_best
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##    mtry min_n .config         
##   <int> <int> <chr>           
## 1    13     5 pre0_mod21_post0
rf_auc_fit <- 
  Health_fit %>% 
  collect_predictions(parameters = rf_best)

Metrics

Confusion Matrix

conf_mat(rf_auc_fit, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class)
##           Truth
## Prediction    No   Yes
##        No  28586   237
##        Yes     1     6

The model correctly classified people with no crohn disease, but is underperforming to predict those who has crohn disease. OUt of people with no crohn disease(N= 28587), the model correctly classified 28586. However, out of 243 people with crohn disease, the model only classified 6 as having crohn disease, showing significant underperformance. #### Accuracy

accuracy(rf_auc_fit, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level= "second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric  .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 accuracy binary         0.992

The accuracy of the model is 99.17, which even though is good but somewhat misleading given the model underperformance for people with crohn disease.

Sensitivity

sens(rf_auc_fit, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level= "second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 sens    binary        0.0247

The sensitivity of the model is only 2.5 % which is very low.

Specificity

spec(rf_auc_fit, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level= "second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 spec    binary         1.000

The specificity of the model is 99.99 % which is good.

F1 score

f_meas(rf_auc_fit, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level= "second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 f_meas  binary         0.048

The F1 score is low, which is 4.8%. This means that the model is failing to coreectly identify positive cases.

Baseline model

rf_baseline <- rand_forest(
  trees = 100,
  mtry = NULL,     
  min_n = NULL
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger") %>%
  set_mode("classification")
Baseline_res <- fit_resamples(
  rf_baseline,
  Crohn ~ ., 
  resamples = folds,
  control=control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE)
)

Collecting the Results

collect_metrics(Baseline_res)
## # A tibble: 3 × 6
##   .metric     .estimator    mean     n  std_err .config        
##   <chr>       <chr>        <dbl> <int>    <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 accuracy    binary     0.992      10 0.000644 pre0_mod0_post0
## 2 brier_class binary     0.00828    10 0.000615 pre0_mod0_post0
## 3 roc_auc     binary     0.632      10 0.0196   pre0_mod0_post0
preds <- collect_predictions(Baseline_res)

Confusion Matrix of baseline model

conf_mat(preds, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class)
##           Truth
## Prediction    No   Yes
##        No  28587   243
##        Yes     0     0

The model correctly classified people with no crohn disease, but is underperforming to predict those who has crohn disease. Out of people with no crohn disease(N= 28587), the model correctly classified 28587. However, out of 243 people with crohn disease, the model didn’t classify any of them as positive, showing significant underperformance.

Accuracy of baseline model

accuracy(preds, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level="second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric  .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>    <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 accuracy binary         0.992

The accurarcy of the model is 99.15% which as we know is misleading given the model didn’t classifies anyone as having the crohn disease.

Sensitivity of baseline model

sens(preds, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class,event_level= "second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 sens    binary             0

The sensitivity of the model is zero.

Specificity of baseline model

spec(preds, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level= "second")
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 spec    binary             1

The specificity of the model is one.

#F1 score of baseline model

f_meas(preds, truth = Crohn,
         estimate = .pred_class, event_level= "second")
## Warning: While computing binary `precision()`, no predicted events were detected (i.e.
## `true_positive + false_positive = 0`).
## Precision is undefined in this case, and `NA` will be returned.
## Note that 243 true event(s) actually occurred for the problematic event level,
## Yes
## # A tibble: 1 × 3
##   .metric .estimator .estimate
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl>
## 1 f_meas  binary            NA

The F1 value is also zero.

Comparing the model

Based on the comparison of the model using the different metrics, we can say that the best model extracted using the hyperparameter tuning is better than the baseline model, even though the best model is also underperforming to predict crohn disease. This indicates that better predictor variables are required and even upsampling can also help in increasing the model performance due to uneven succes to failure ratio.

Final Model

final_model <- finalize_model(
                  rf_model,
                  rf_best
                )

final_model
## Random Forest Model Specification (classification)
## 
## Main Arguments:
##   mtry = 13
##   trees = 100
##   min_n = 5
## 
## Engine-Specific Arguments:
##   num.threads = cores
## 
## Computational engine: ranger

Variable Importance

tree_prep <- prep(Health_recipe)

final_model %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "permutation") %>%
  fit(Crohn ~ .,
    data = juice(tree_prep)) %>%
  vip(geom = "point")

final_rf_workflow <- workflow() %>%
                      add_recipe(Health_recipe) %>%
                      add_model(final_model)

final_results <- final_rf_workflow %>%
                    last_fit(cv_split)

final_results %>%
  collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
##   .metric     .estimator .estimate .config        
##   <chr>       <chr>          <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 accuracy    binary       0.992   pre0_mod0_post0
## 2 roc_auc     binary       0.564   pre0_mod0_post0
## 3 brier_class binary       0.00883 pre0_mod0_post0
